title: Hennepin County Opens Criminal Investigation Into 17 Federal Agent Incidents
excerpt: Hennepin County Attorney Mary Moriarty announced a criminal investigation into 17 incidents of potentially unlawful conduct by federal immigration agents during Operation Metro Surge, including the deaths of Renee Good and Alex Pretti, and created a public evidence portal.
content:
On March 2, 2026, Hennepin County Attorney Mary Moriarty announced that her office was investigating 17 alleged incidents of excessive or potentially criminal conduct by federal agents during Operation Metro Surge. The office created a public portal for residents to submit video and documentary evidence.

The incidents under investigation include the January 7 fatal shooting of Renee Good, the January 24 fatal shooting of VA nurse Alex Pretti, and a January 21 incident in which then-Border Patrol Commander-at-Large Gregory Bovino was captured on camera tossing a tear gas canister toward protesters in a Minneapolis park. Federal agents blocked Minnesota state investigators from accessing evidence in both shooting cases, hampering the county's investigation.

  • Moriarty stated: "We will investigate and pursue charging where appropriate"
  • FBI had cut the Minnesota Bureau of Criminal Apprehension out of the Renee Good shooting investigation in January, denying state investigators access to forensic evidence
  • Criminology experts cited the pattern as evidence that federal agents were "poorly trained, poorly supervised, and that their agency and leadership have sent a message to them that these kinds of actions are acceptable"
